_**Agent (claude-fable-5 2026-06-12 12:02:47Z)**_

Done. The repo is pushed to github.com/lycfyi/presentation-20260612 on the `main` branch.

What happened along the way:

- Committed the pending `.specstory` history files first, since the working tree wasn't clean.
- Renamed the local `master` branch to `main` and added the remote.
- Outbound SSH on port 22 to GitHub is blocked on this network, so I switched the remote URL to `ssh://git@ssh.github.com:443/...` (GitHub's SSH-over-HTTPS endpoint) and pushed through that. The remote stays on this URL, so future pushes will work without any extra steps.

Since this was the first branch pushed to the new repo, GitHub set `main` as the default branch automatically — no `master` exists on the remote.
